title: Subsets
prompt:
Given a list of unique integers `nums`, return **all possible subsets** (the power set). Order doesn't matter for grading — subsets and their contents will be compared regardless of order.

**Example**: `subsets([1,2,3])` → all 8 subsets, including `[]` and `[1,2,3]`.
hint_notes: Classic backtracking template: choose (append), explore (recurse with start=i+1), un-choose (pop). Record the current path at every node of the recursion, not just at the leaves. Bit-mask iteration (0..2^n-1) is an equivalent non-recursive approach worth mentioning. Tag: backtracking-choose-explore-unchoose.
